quick_skim.title: Learning Interface Breakup: A Geometry-Conditioned Latent Surrogate for Spray Formation
quick_skim.abstract: Designing spray nozzles requires predicting how geometry shapes transient two-phase breakup, but high-fidelity volume-of-fluid (VOF) simulations with adaptive mesh refinement (AMR) are too expensive for iterative design exploration. Standard surrogate models are also challenged by this setting because both the liquid--gas interface and the underlying adaptive discretization evolve across time and geometries. We introduce a geometry-conditioned latent surrogate trained on 797 two-phase nozzle simulations that addresses this by encoding the AMR cell-density field, rather than the full multi-channel flow state, as a compact proxy for where the solver concentrates resolution. From this representation, the model reconstructs transient density evolution and nozzle geometry, and a lightweight second stage recovers the remaining flow variables. On held-out simulations, the method accurately captures key interface dynamics while reducing inference time to 0.045 seconds per trajectory, corresponding to a speed-up of more than $6\times10^4$ relative to Basilisk CFD. These results suggest that AMR refinement structure can serve as a compact and learnable representation for geometry-conditioned surrogate modeling of transient two-phase flows.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Uses two-phase VOF simulations with AMR for liquid-gas interface breakup
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用两相VOF模拟与自适应网格细化研究液-气界面破碎
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用两相VOF模拟与自适应网格细化研究液-气界面破碎
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用两相VOF模拟与自适应网格细化研究液-气界面破碎
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Develops a geometry-conditioned latent surrogate for two-phase nozzle spray breakup using VOF data, encoding AMR cell density to reconstruct transient interface evolution, enabling fast design exploration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对喷雾喷嘴设计中两相界面破碎的高保真VOF模拟计算成本高的问题，提出了一种几何条件潜在替代模型。通过编码自适应网格细化（AMR）的单元密度场代替完整流场状态，从该表示中重建瞬态界面演化，在797次两相喷嘴模拟数据上训练后，能够快速预测不同几何条件下的界面破碎过程，为加速迭代设计探索提供了高效工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究针对喷雾喷嘴设计中两相界面破碎的高保真VOF模拟计算成本高的问题，提出了一种几何条件潜在替代模型。通过编码自适应网格细化（AMR）的单元密度场代替完整流场状态，从该表示中重建瞬态界面演化，在797次两相喷嘴模拟数据上训练后，能够快速预测不同几何条件下的界面破碎过程，为加速迭代设计探索提供了高效工具。

quick_skim.title: A hybrid sharp-diffuse interface approach to accurately model melt pool dynamics with rapid evaporation in laser-based processing of metals
quick_skim.abstract: Predictive simulation of melt pool dynamics in laser-based processing of metals, e.g., laser beam welding or laser powder bed fusion additive manufacturing, requires accurate resolution of thermo-hydrodynamic interactions at the melt-gas interface. Here, evaporation-induced recoil pressure and temperature-dependent surface tension govern the flow. Because these mechanisms depend sensitively, often exponentially, on the interface temperature, reliable predictions demand highly accurate heat transfer models. Popular diffuse-interface formulations smear the extreme thermal gradients as typical for laser-metal interactions, leading to interface temperature errors that critically degrade the accuracy of interface force predictions and melt pool dynamics. We present a hybrid sharp-diffuse interface approach for high-fidelity modelling of melt pool thermo-hydrodynamics with rapid evaporation. The heat transfer problem is represented using a sharp-interface unfitted finite element (CutFEM) formulation, enabling accurate prediction of the temperature field. The multi-phase flow problem, characterized by large density ratios and complex interface dynamics, is accurately captured using a robust level-set-based one-fluid diffuse-interface finite element formulation. Consistent coupling is achieved by extending the sharp-interface temperature into a narrow interface region to evaluate temperature-dependent interface forces within the diffuse-interface flow framework. In practically relevant benchmarks, the sharp-interface thermal model exhibits second-order spatial convergence, enabling finite element sizes two orders of magnitude larger than the diffuse-interface approach for 1 accuracy. In a novel coupled thermo-hydrodynamic benchmark representative of laser-metal interactions, the hybrid approach is one order of magnitude more accurate than a purely diffuse-interface model on the same mesh. quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: hybrid sharp-diffuse interface method for accurate interfacial heat transfer, transferable to falling film evaporation modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 混合尖锐-扩散界面方法精确处理界面换热，可迁移至降膜蒸发模拟
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 混合尖锐-扩散界面方法精确处理界面换热，可迁移至降膜蒸发模拟
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 混合尖锐-扩散界面方法精确处理界面换热，可迁移至降膜蒸发模拟
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a hybrid sharp-diffuse interface approach to resolve melt pool evaporation with high interface temperature accuracy, providing a method that could improve falling film evaporation and heat transfer simulations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对激光加工中蒸发驱动熔池模拟的界面温度精度问题，本文提出混合尖锐-扩散界面方法，结合尖锐界面热通量条件与扩散界面捕捉。该方法准确解析了回流压力与表面张力，其对相变换热的处理思路可迁移至垂直管降膜蒸发的CFD建模。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对激光加工中蒸发驱动熔池模拟的界面温度精度问题，本文提出混合尖锐-扩散界面方法，结合尖锐界面热通量条件与扩散界面捕捉。该方法准确解析了回流压力与表面张力，其对相变换热的处理思路可迁移至垂直管降膜蒸发的CFD建模。
